sections.summary: This summary provides key information about the EuroStable asset-referenced token (ART). It is intended to support the prospective holder in making an informed decision. The summary covers the issuer and its authorisation, the reference assets and stabilisation mechanism, the rights of holders including the redemption right at par, the principal risks, the reserve composition and segregation, the technology, and the redemption procedure. The full white paper, including the management body statement, must be read alongside this summary and is the controlling document for the offer.
sections.risk_warning: The crypto-asset described in this white paper may lose its value in part or in full, may not always be transferable, and may not be liquid. Past performance of the EuroStable ART is not indicative of future performance. The crypto-asset is not covered by an investor compensation scheme or a deposit guarantee scheme. The risk of total loss is real and the holder should not invest amounts that cannot be lost in full.
sections.management_statement: The management body of the issuer declares that this white paper complies with the requirements of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 (MiCAR), that the information presented is fair, clear, and not misleading, and that no information has been omitted that would be material to the assessment of the asset-referenced token. The management body assumes responsibility for the content of this white paper.
sections.notification_date: Date of notification to BaFin: 1 June 2026.
sections.language: This white paper is drawn up in English, a language customary in the sphere of international finance, and a German-language translation is available upon request from the issuer.
sections.issuer: EuroStable AG is the issuer of the asset-referenced token. The legal form of the issuer is a stock corporation (Aktiengesellschaft) organised under the laws of the Federal Republic of Germany, with its registered seat and business address at Neuer Wall 80, 20354 Hamburg, registered with the commercial register of the Local Court of Hamburg under HRB 174562. The issuer holds an authorisation as an ART issuer under Art. 21 MiCAR granted by BaFin on 15 March 2026. The management body consists of three members with combined prior experience in regulated payment services, treasury management, and information security.
sections.art: The EuroStable ART is an asset-referenced token within the meaning of Art. 3(1)(6) MiCAR. The reference assets consist of a basket of EUR-denominated short-dated sovereign debt instruments issued by Member States of the euro area and cash deposits at credit institutions authorised in the European Union. The basket composition is fixed within a defined target band and is rebalanced on a quarterly cycle. The stabilisation mechanism operates through one-to-one issuance and redemption against the reference value, daily reconciliation of the reserve composition against the outstanding supply, and an intra-day arbitrage channel reserved for authorised participants.
sections.offer_or_admission: The offer is addressed to professional and eligible counterparty customers in selected European Union jurisdictions and excludes retail offerings in jurisdictions where the issuer has not completed the relevant passporting notification. The issuance is conducted at the reference value plus a fixed subscription fee. The target market includes regulated payment institutions, electronic money institutions, and credit institutions seeking on-chain settlement. Admission to trading on a regulated crypto-asset trading platform may follow at the discretion of the issuer.
sections.rights_and_obligations: The holder of the EuroStable ART has the following rights against the issuer: a contractual right of redemption at par at any time in accordance with Art. 39 MiCAR, a right to file complaints under the procedure described in Section 9 of this white paper, and information rights regarding the composition of the reserve. The obligations of the holder include compliance with applicable transfer restrictions, sanctions screening, and the issuer's terms of service. The redemption right may not be waived by contract and is enforceable in the courts of the Federal Republic of Germany.
sections.technology: The EuroStable ART is issued on a permissioned protocol based on a Byzantine fault tolerant consensus mechanism. Smart contract modules govern issuance, redemption, and supply control, and were audited by two independent code security firms in February 2026 and April 2026. Custody of the underlying technical keys is provided by a regulated qualified custodian in Liechtenstein, with key management based on multi-party computation and segregated cold storage. The issuer publishes a monthly attestation of the on-chain supply.
sections.risks: The principal risks attached to the EuroStable ART include the following. Market risk arises on the reserve assets and may cause the reserve value to fall below the aggregate par value of the outstanding tokens. Credit risk arises on the reserve custodian and on each reserve counterparty, including in the event of insolvency or sanctions designation. Liquidity risk arises during a redemption surge if reserve assets cannot be converted to cash at par within the contractual settlement window. De-peg risk arises if the secondary market price of the token diverges from the reference value. Operational risk arises from smart contract failure, key compromise, validator failure, and incident response shortcomings. Regulatory risk arises from changes in MiCAR Level 2 standards, EBA guidance, and BaFin supervisory practice. Reserve impairment events trigger the cure procedure described in Section 11. The redemption right may be temporarily suspended only in narrowly defined operational or regulatory emergencies and never to manage commercial outcomes.
sections.reserve_of_assets: The reserve of assets is composed of short-dated EUR-denominated sovereign debt issued by Member States of the euro area with residual maturity of less than ninety days, and of overnight cash deposits at credit institutions authorised in the European Union. The composition of the reserve is fixed within a defined target band and rebalanced on a quarterly cycle. The reserve is held by a separate special-purpose vehicle and is segregated from the issuer's own balance sheet by way of a security trust structure. Custody is provided by a Tier-1 European credit institution acting as qualified custodian under a tri-party custody and segregation agreement. The valuation of the reserve is performed daily at fair value by the issuer's middle office and verified weekly by the custodian. The reserve is audited monthly by an independent statutory auditor and reconciled against the outstanding token supply on a continuous basis through automated supply oracles. Any shortfall triggers the cure procedure described in Section 11 and a mandatory report to BaFin within twenty-four hours.
sections.environmental_impact: The consensus mechanism used by the issuance protocol is a permissioned Byzantine fault tolerant variant that does not rely on proof-of-work and does not consume energy at the level of public proof-of-work chains. The issuer monitors the energy consumption of the validator infrastructure on a continuous basis and publishes annual environmental and climate-related impact data in line with the ESMA regulatory technical standards on sustainability indicators for distributed ledger technology. Validators source electricity from the European mainland grid, with a contracted renewable share that is reported alongside the annual disclosure. The methodology, scope, and limitations of the climate-related disclosures are described in Annex 2.
sections.redemption: The redemption right is exercisable at any time at par against the holder of record. The procedure requires a KYC-verified redemption request submitted through the issuer's portal. The timing of settlement is one business day from receipt of a complete redemption request, with cut-off at 16:00 CET for same-cycle processing. Redemption is settled by EUR bank transfer to the holder's verified bank account. Redemption fees are limited to the reserve transaction costs and do not exceed twenty basis points; fees are disclosed in advance through the issuer's portal. The redemption right may be temporarily suspended only in narrowly defined operational or regulatory emergencies as described in Section 11, with the suspension reported to BaFin within twenty-four hours and to the public on the issuer's portal.
